figures.caption: Fig. 3: Centralized map sharing with five agents where with small local maps, the global map and sharing size grow rapidly.

Fig. 4: Illustration of five robots’ local map beliefs with the limited-sharing algorithm. The top row indicates the collision risk heat map. The bottom row shows the robot’s safe path to the next waypoints in each robot’s local map belief.
Fig. 5: Comparison of local 3DGS maps sharing for two agents with centralized and limited-sharing algorithms.
Fig. 2: (a) Planned safe trajectory with the corresponding mask. (b) Each robot proposes an informative view for itself and for its teammate from its local 3DGS map.

Fig. 1: System overview of the distributed, information-driven, and risk-aware multi-robot NBV framework. In a team of R agents, each agent constructs a local 3DGS map from RGB-D observations. Agents exchange future trajectory and EIG scores over masked regions along trajectories in their local maps. Viewpoints are selected to maximize risk-aware information gain, after which each robot executes the corresponding actions.
